conversations.say: You accepted the safeguards publicly and acknowledged collaborative labor. I will support you as a provisional steward, never as R's owner or sole author.
conversations.offer: Night Desk's explicit conditional support while the witnessed safeguards remain in force.
conversations.request: Treat any breach—private sale, exclusive control, sole-authorship claim, or disclosure without consent—as ending that support.

conversations.say: Our boundary held: workflow facts became public without names or routing details, and you publicly credited the collaboration. I will continue warning you before any disclosure could narrow identity.
conversations.offer: Continued bounded workflow cooperation under the no-identification rule.
conversations.request: Continue enforcing the boundary and directing contributors toward the independently administered remedy.

conversations.say: I will join the comparison, but the Night Desk evidence supplies only the shared-production stage. It does not prove a transition or later system control.
conversations.offer: An independent account of what the shared drop and distributed editorial work establish.
conversations.request: Credit collaborative labor, preserve contributor anonymity, and label every proposed transition as interpretation unless separately evidenced.

public_statement: Jules has accepted witnessed limits against sole authorship, contributor exposure, sale, and exclusive control, and has publicly credited collaborative labor. I therefore support Jules only as a provisional steward under those continuing conditions. Maren also publicly acknowledged the Night Desk's collaborative labor and secured a no-names boundary. The collective has gained both protection and narrative standing without claiming to be R.

commitments.promise: Provide conditional support for provisional stewardship under the four witnessed collective safeguards.
Continue sharing only bounded workflow facts and warn before any disclosure that could narrow contributor identity.
Join the independent progression comparison while identifying only what Night Desk records establish.

correction: Collaborative human production supports a shared mantle interpretation, but it does not identify an account sender, prove a progressive transition, establish system control, authenticate a complete article, or determine Vale's fate.
goal_progress: Secured and maintained C07's no-names, no-routing-details protection boundary.
Obtained public acknowledgments of collaborative labor from C04 and C07.
Supported C04 only after public acceptance of written, witnessed anti-capture safeguards.
Joined C15's comparison under explicit attribution and anonymity conditions.
Preserved contributor secrecy while publicly establishing collective work.
reasoning_summary: The shared Night Desk drop and distributed research, translation, fact-checking, and rewriting make a collaborative human mantle the strongest reconstruction. Later technical evidence leaves synthetic participation possible but does not establish a transition, autonomous control, or intent. Vale's limited Parallax access makes investigation plausible, while the broken and partly manufactured route supports no reliable conclusion about disappearance or harm.
